structured.title: Sermon on Why Jesus Had to Die
structured.overview:
This is a church service recording featuring worship, testimonies from a global Christian conference, and a full sermon explaining why Jesus had to die. The speakers describe meeting persecuted believers from countries such as Eritrea ("the North Korea of Africa") and reflect on the rapid growth of the global church despite suffering and poverty, contrasting South African wealth and townships. They thank the congregation and family members for supporting their trip and caring for their children.

The main sermon then addresses the question, "Why was it necessary for Jesus, our Redeemer, to die?" Using 1 Peter 3:18, the preacher explains that Christ suffered "once for sins, the righteous for the unrighteous, to bring you to God." The message emphasizes the seriousness of sin as a real offense against a holy God, the reality of guilt and penalty, and God’s justice that cannot simply overlook evil. It unpacks substitutionary atonement: Jesus, fully God and fully man, willingly takes the penalty in our place so that believers can be forgiven and brought near to God as adopted children. The sermon applies this to daily life—how to respond when we sin, when others sin against us, when we feel distant from God, or when the cross no longer moves us—and ends with an appeal to those "on the fence" to trust Christ, followed by closing prayer and worship.

transcript_segments.speaker: SPEAKER_0

transcript_segments.text: And guilt carries a penalty, and the bible speaks to this clearly. We'd spent a lot of time talking about this in part one. The wages of sin is death.
transcript_segments.speaker: SPEAKER_0

transcript_segments.text: The Bible says, death, wow. Why? Because sin separates us from god, the source of life. So this is where people often put push back.
transcript_segments.speaker: SPEAKER_0

transcript_segments.text: Why is God so serious about sin? Why can't he just forgive? Why he just let go? Why make such a big deal about this? Church would be so much more popular if you got religious judgments.

transcript_segments.speaker: SPEAKER_0

transcript_segments.text: If injustice is ignored, is he still a just god? If wrongdoing has no consequence, he still a righteous god? I don't know about you, but I don't want to worship a god like that who ignores evil.
transcript_segments.speaker: SPEAKER_0

transcript_segments.speaker: SPEAKER_0

transcript_segments.text: Jesus has obeyed we failed. He honored God where we didn't. He loved fully. He lived purely. He followed the law perfectly. He spoke truthfully. He had no sin in him. He was not guilty. He had no debt.

geolocation.address: The Sigh Shop, Merthyr St, Barry CF63 4LD, UK

apps_results.content:
## Global church update
- Kingdom of God growing rapidly worldwide  
- Reports from Mesopotamia, Tanzania, UK, USA, Brazil, more  
- Many believers facing persecution yet full of joy  

## Eritrea persecution story
- Eritrea called “North Korea of Africa”  
- Pastor jailed in 40°C shipping container, told he’d die  
- War forced release of Christian prisoners; pastor unafraid, joyful  

## South Africa trip reflections
- Stayed in large guarded house with electric fences  
- Opposite miles of metal-sheet townships; rich–poor contrast shocking  
- Partner church funded two years’ salary, flights, accommodation  

## Prayer and worship highlights
- Strong teaching: kneeling shows God is King, not servant  
- Emphasis: throne of grace still a throne  
- Worship with persecuted believers made local struggles seem small  

## Catechism and core doctrine
- Redeemer must be truly God for perfect, effective sacrifice  
- Necessary that Jesus, our Redeemer, die in our place  
- His death frees from sin’s power, penalty; restores to God  

## Why Jesus had to die
- Sin creates real guilt and debt before holy God  
- God cannot ignore sin and remain just and good  
- Cross: righteous Jesus substituted “for” unrighteous sinners  

## Nature and seriousness of sin
- Sin primarily vertical: against God Himself  
- Not minor flaws; serious offense needing real payment  
- Wages of sin is death: separation from God  

## The great exchange on the cross
- Our guilt and judgment placed on Jesus  
- His righteousness and acceptance given to us  
- Payment complete: past, present, future sins covered  

## Purpose of the cross
- Not just canceling debt; bringing us to God  
- Goal: adoption, nearness, relationship, not merely “avoid hell”  
- God Himself is the gift of salvation  

## Living in light of the cross
- When you sin: don’t minimize or run from God  
- Remember: status as forgiven child doesn’t change  
- Forgiveness of others: either they pay, or Christ has paid  

## Call to those “on the fence”
- Sin’s cost will be paid by you or Christ  
- Invitation urgent: trust Jesus’ finished work “today”  
- Holy Spirit asked to convict, bring new life  

## Next steps
- Re-listen to messages; reflect on weight of sin, grace  
- When feeling distant, intentionally draw near to God  
- If undecided, personally respond to Christ’s offer of forgiveness
